title: Authorization chain absent — no sign-off owner, kill-switch, safe-words or abort criteria
evidence_observation: Planning describes Scope, Threat Actors, Scenarios Description and CODENAME but contains no Rules of Engagement section, no named sign-off authority, no kill-switch / abort criteria, no safe-word convention, and no escalation contact list. Tier 1.2 work has not yet landed in this SOP.
evidence_expected: An 'Authorization & Rules of Engagement' subsection should name: who signs off (Local CISO, Application owner, RT Lead); what triggers an immediate halt (production impact, data exposure, real incident overlap); the safe-word or coded signal used; and the contact list (RT PoC, Blue Team PoC, escalation chain).
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement Interactions — Rules of Engagement; NIST-800-115:NIST SP 800-115 §3 Planning (RoE, authorization); TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 §Preparation — engagement letter / kill-switch (general); DORA:DORA Art.6 / Art.24 (general) — ICT risk management and operational testing controls; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — risk management of the test (general)
recommendation: Add an 'Authorization & Rules of Engagement' subsection to Planning, covering sign-off owner, abort criteria, kill-switch, safe-words and escalation contacts. This is the Tier 1.2 fix flagged at the prior run.

title: Authorization chain incomplete — no documented sign-off, kill-switch or abort criteria
evidence_observation: WT is 'mandated to start, stop and approve attacks' (Teams & Responsibilities) and Data Handling references 'WT Point of Contact', but the SOP contains no Rules-of-Engagement subsection, no explicit sign-off owner per Operation, no abort criteria (production impact, data exposure, real incident overlap), no kill-switch / safe-word convention, and no escalation contact list.
evidence_expected: An 'Authorization & Rules of Engagement' subsection should be added to Research & Preparation, naming the sign-off authority, abort triggers, kill-switch procedure, safe-word and escalation chain. This is the Tier 1.2 fix flagged after the prior run.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement Interactions — Rules of Engagement; NIST-800-115:NIST SP 800-115 §3 Planning — authorization, RoE; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — Preparation phase: engagement letter / risk management of the test
recommendation: Add a dedicated 'Authorization & Rules of Engagement' subsection to Research & Preparation (Tier 1.2). Reference the standard Euronext RoE template once it exists.

title: Authorization chain still incomplete — no sign-off owner, kill-switch or abort criteria
evidence_observation: Initiation phase describes scoping and WT selection but contains no Rules of Engagement section, no named sign-off authority, no abort criteria (production impact, data exposure, real incident overlap), and no kill-switch / safe-word convention. WT is 'mandated to start, stop and approve attacks' but procedure for invoking the stop is undefined.
evidence_expected: An 'Authorization & Rules of Engagement' subsection should be added to Initiation. Tier 1.2 work has not yet landed.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement Interactions — Rules of Engagement; NIST-800-115:NIST SP 800-115 §3 Planning; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 §Preparation — engagement letter / risk-management of test; DORA-TLPT:DORA Art.26-27 (general) — competent authority engagement and attestation; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — TCT engagement
recommendation: Add an Authorization & RoE subsection covering sign-off owner, abort criteria, kill-switch procedure, safe-word and escalation chain (Tier 1.2).

title: Methodology has no authorization-chain section — no methodology-level RoE template, kill-switch, or signoff model
evidence_observation: Methodology covers OPSEC, IoCs, OPLOGS, Threat Planning and Scoping but contains no Authorization / Rules of Engagement / kill-switch section. Each engagement SOP is then free to omit it (and currently does). 'Forbidden Attacks' is a partial proxy but is OPSEC-flavoured, not authorization-flavoured.
evidence_expected: Methodology should define a standard RoE template, the sign-off chain (and who can stop an engagement), abort criteria, kill-switch and safe-word convention — so engagement SOPs can reference rather than re-state it. This is the Tier 1.2 keystone.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement Interactions — Rules of Engagement; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 §Preparation — engagement letter / risk management of test (general); NIST-800-115:NIST SP 800-115 §3 Planning
recommendation: Add an 'Authorization & Rules of Engagement' section to the methodology that the SOPs reference. This is the keystone of Tier 1.2.

title: Deck displays 'TLPT RED' classification but no supporting TLPT artefacts are present in the corpus
evidence_observation: Slides 8, 9, 10 and 11 are stamped 'TLPT RED \ confidential' — yet the corpus contains no Control Team charter, no independent TI provider artefact, no test summary / replay / attestation, and no lead-overseer notification record for the operations described.
evidence_expected: If material is marked TLPT, the underlying engagement must satisfy TIBER-EU v2 and DORA Art.26-27 + the RTS on TLPT: independent TI provider, Control Team distinct from blue team, test summary report, remediation plan, attestation. Otherwise the marking should be removed or rephrased (e.g. 'internal red-team — confidential').
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — Closure phase artefacts (test summary report, replay, attestation); DORA-TLPT:Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 Art.26-27 + RTS on TLPT (TI provider, control team, attestation)
recommendation: Either downgrade the classification banner on these slides if the engagements were not TLPT, or attach the regulator-mandated artefact set (TTI report, attestation, lead-overseer notice) to the operation summaries.

title: Data-handling rules absent from Threat Scenarios SOP
evidence_observation: Operations and Exercises SOPs (v1.5 / v1.6) each contain a 'Data Handling' subsection covering PIA categories, halt-on-encounter behaviour and WT escalation. Threat Scenarios v1.6 has no equivalent section even though Threat Scenarios touch internal SaaS, secret stores and ticketing where personal/regulated data is plausible.
evidence_expected: Add a 'Data Handling' subsection matching the wording in Operations_Process v1.5 (sensitive-data halt + WT PoC contact + delete-or-return).
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement — Handling of Sensitive Information; DORA:DORA general — data protection during testing (verify clause)
recommendation: Lift the Data Handling section from Operations_Process v1.5 into Threat Scenarios v1.7, tailoring the examples to internal-tool walkthroughs (e.g. CI/CD pipelines may contain regulated build artefacts).

title: No documented kill-switch / abort criteria — would fail DORA risk-management-of-testing examination
evidence_observation: WT is 'mandated to start, stop and approve attacks' but no abort criteria are defined; nothing in the SOP triggers an immediate halt for production-impact or real-incident overlap.
evidence_expected: DORA Chapter IV requires that operational-resilience tests (whether TLPT or basic) have documented risk-management, including kill-switch and rollback. TIBER-EU v2 likewise expects safety controls in the Preparation phase.
framework_refs: DORA:DORA Art.24 (general) — risk management of operational resilience testing; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — risk management of the test (general)
recommendation: Tier 1.2: document kill-switch criteria and procedure in the new Authorization & RoE subsection (see lifecycle finding -003).

title: TIBER-EU Preparation actors (Control Team, RT Provider, TI Provider) not named in Initiation
evidence_observation: Initiation introduces RT, WT, BT and mentions TI Team in Teams & Responsibilities, but does not refer to TIBER-EU-mandated actors when the Exercise is run as TLPT: Control Team (distinct from BT and WT), independent Threat Intelligence Provider, lead-overseer / competent-authority engagement.
evidence_expected: When run as TLPT, Initiation should establish the Control Team, procure the TI Provider (independent of the RT Provider per RTS on TLPT), and engage the lead overseer / competent authority. Closure already names these actors — Initiation should too.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — Preparation phase actors (TCT, CT, TI provider, RT provider, WT); DORA-TLPT:DORA Art.27 + RTS on TLPT — tester / TI-provider requirements (verify live text)
recommendation: Add a 'TLPT alignment' callout to Initiation listing the Control Team, TI Provider and lead-overseer engagement when the Exercise is run as TLPT. Verify clause references against the live RTS on TLPT.

title: No codename convention named for Physical Pentest engagements
evidence_observation: Doc never instructs Operators to assign a codename to the Physical Pentest, although the methodology says every engagement gets a codename and 'Operations and Exercises' follow the fictional/historical character theme.
evidence_expected: Either (a) require a codename per RedTeam_Methodology v0.8 §Codename and state which theme applies, or (b) explicitly note that Physical Pentests inherit the codename of the parent Exercise/Operation when nested under one.
framework_refs: OTHER:RedTeam_Methodology v0.8 §Codename
recommendation: Add a 'Codename' bullet to Planning saying the Physical Pentest receives a codename per the team theme (standalone) or inherits the parent engagement's codename.

section_ref: Planning / Get out of jail letter
title: Authorization letter present but operator safety controls (kill-switch, safe-word, abort criteria) absent
evidence_observation: 'In a worst case scenario where, during the tests, local personnel get suspicious of the Red Team Operators activities and threaten to take actions, such as calling the police... the Red Team Operators need to show the Get out of jail letter to the person...'
evidence_expected: Beyond the letter, operator safety needs: (a) a kill-switch / safe-word for stopping the engagement immediately, (b) an abort criteria list (operator detained, physical altercation, unexpected restricted area, real incident overlap), (c) a duress / check-in protocol with the Local CISO. Tier 1.2 work has not landed here either.
framework_refs: OSSTMM:OSSTMM §Operational Security — safety controls; PTES:PTES Pre-engagement — Rules of Engagement; NIST-800-115:NIST SP 800-115 §6 Physical security testing — safety considerations; DORA:DORA Art.24 (general) — risk management of operational resilience testing; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — risk management of the test (general)
recommendation: Add an 'Operator Safety & Abort Criteria' subsection to Planning covering kill-switch, safe-word, abort criteria, duress protocol, and a regular check-in cadence with the Local CISO. This is the Tier 1.2 fix flagged at the prior run.

title: RedTeaming_v1.0.pptx is content-identical to RedTeaming_v0.4.pptx minus the Scheduling chapter — likely older / shorter twin
evidence_observation: diff shows v1.0 differs from v0.4 only by removal of agenda lines 'Scheduling / Phases of RT Process / Teams Composition & Duties / Detailed Phases / Complete Schedule'. All scheduling slides (10-15) are still present in v1.0, but they are no longer announced in the agenda. Filename version (v1.0) is HIGHER than v0.4 yet content is LESS complete.
evidence_expected: Either (a) the two decks are different artefacts that should be clearly named and version-differentiated (e.g. 'RedTeaming_Exec_Overview_v1.0' for the exec-summary cut), or (b) v1.0 is a stale duplicate and should be retired in favour of v0.4. The current state confuses the corpus and triggers the input-selection rule ('analyse only the highest version') incorrectly.
framework_refs: OTHER:Internal corpus consistency / Input selection rule
recommendation: Rename one of the two decks to disambiguate (purpose-suffix), or retire the duplicate. Recommend treating the more-complete v0.4 as canonical until a refreshed v1.x deck is authored from the methodology v0.8 source.

title: AET role referenced but not defined in this doc or any engagement SOP
evidence_observation: 'Access to Euronext BloodHound tenant is fully managed by AET, and access to it is restricted to members of the team.'
evidence_expected: AET (Assessment & Exploitation Team) is on the corpus orphan-actor list (references/corpus_vocabulary.yaml). Either define AET in this doc or in the methodology, and clarify the relationship to the Red Team.
framework_refs: OTHER:Internal corpus consistency (references/corpus_vocabulary.yaml orphan_actors §AET)
recommendation: Either expand AET on first use in this doc or in RedTeam_Methodology Glossary.

title: Adjacent-remit work (threat hunting at close-out, during external engagements, during live incidents) is not described
evidence_observation: Methodology lists OPSEC, OPLOGS, Threat Planning, Scoping — but does not describe the team's role in: (a) post-engagement threat hunting, (b) supporting external red-team exercises run against Euronext, (c) supporting live incident response. References/corpus_vocabulary.yaml flags these as in-scope for the team's actual remit.
evidence_expected: Methodology should have an 'Adjacent Responsibilities' section covering these three activities, even if briefly, so the team's broader role is regulator-defensible.
framework_refs: DORA:DORA Art.17/24 (general) — incident management and operational-resilience testing; OTHER:schemas/engagement-types.md §Adjacent team responsibilities
recommendation: Add an 'Adjacent Responsibilities' section describing threat-hunt close-out, external-engagement support and incident-response support.

title: No Control Team / TI Provider / Lead Overseer references anywhere in the methodology
evidence_observation: Methodology lists Red Team, Blue Team, White Team and Threat Intelligence — but no Control Team, no TIBER Cyber Team, no lead-overseer / competent-authority engagement.
evidence_expected: Even for an internal-by-default methodology, the role taxonomy that applies when an engagement is escalated to TLPT should be documented so it can be invoked without re-writing the methodology.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — actors taxonomy; DORA-TLPT:DORA Art.27 + RTS on TLPT — Control Team, TI Provider (verify live text)
recommendation: Add a 'TLPT role mapping' subsection naming TCT, CT, TI Provider, RT Provider, WT and the financial entity, with notes on how each maps to existing Euronext teams.

title: Exercises described as 'well-planned and aligned with TIBER-EU' but methodology never expands what that alignment entails
evidence_observation: 'Red Team Exercises, are full Red Team engagements, well-planned and aligned with TIBER-EU. They are design to mimic a specific Threat Actor TTPs...'
evidence_expected: If the methodology claims TIBER-EU alignment, it should name the four TIBER-EU v2 phases (Preparation, Threat Intelligence, Red Team Test, Closure) and the regulator-mandated actors (TIBER Cyber Team, Control Team, TI Provider, RT Provider, White Team). Currently this claim is unsupported in the body.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — phases and actors; DORA-TLPT:DORA Art.26-27 + RTS on TLPT (verify live text)
recommendation: Add a 'Regulatory Mapping' section explaining TIBER-EU and DORA TLPT alignment: the four TIBER-EU phases, the regulator-mandated actors, when an Exercise qualifies as TLPT, and what additional artefacts must be produced. Verify clause references against live regulation.

title: Sensitive findings recorded in deck slide rather than a tracked remediation backlog
evidence_observation: Slide 8 records action items ('No ETA for now', 'SSH keys ... 1st plan for ssh keys in tools team') without owner, target date or remediation tracking ID.
evidence_expected: Per Purple Teaming Process v0.1 Outputs & Tracking section, every gap should produce a remediation-backlog entry with named owner, severity and target date in the tracking forum (RT Research Repository or security risk register).
framework_refs: OTHER:Purple Teaming Process v0.1 §Outputs & Tracking
recommendation: Migrate slide-8 items into the tracking forum chosen by the Purple Teaming process and reference Jira/Confluence IDs from this slide.

title: Operation post-mortems (Phantom Signature / Crimson Breach / Fight Club) lack purple-team and threat-hunt handover artefacts
evidence_observation: Each operation slide ends with 'Next steps – consolidate change management CIs and Inerente Assets on Applicantion & Components' but does not capture (a) detection-vs-action map, (b) hunt-hypothesis list, (c) named remediation owners, or (d) Purple Team close-out evidence required by Purple_Teaming_Process_v0.1.docx.
evidence_expected: Each operation summary should link to the close-out artefact set defined by the Purple Teaming Process — detection-vs-action map, gap register, hunt-hypothesis list, OFI register, remediation backlog.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Post Exploitation / Reporting; MITRE-ATTACK:ATT&CK-based reporting
recommendation: Either (a) link each operation slide to its Purple Team close-out output set, or (b) refactor the dossier so operation post-mortems live in a separate engagement-results register and are not interleaved with threat-actor reference material.

title: Operation post-mortems lack DORA-style closure artefacts (test summary, attestation, regulator notification)
evidence_observation: Phantom Signature, Crimson Breach and Fight Club operation slides describe objective, conclusion and a single 'Next steps' note — no test summary report, no joint attestation, no remediation tracking IDs, no record of competent-authority engagement.
evidence_expected: If any of these operations were treated as TLPT, DORA Art.26 + RTS on TLPT closure requirements apply: test report, remediation plan, attestation to the lead overseer / competent authority, and mutual-recognition handling. If they are internal Operations only (not TLPT), the 'TLPT RED' banner should be removed.
framework_refs: DORA-TLPT:DORA Art.26 (general) — closure artefacts and authority engagement; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — Closure (test report, attestation)
recommendation: Clarify the regulatory status of each historical operation; for any that should have been TLPT, retrospectively capture the test report and attestation. Verify wording against the live DORA RTS on TLPT.

title: Physical scope not explicitly addressed (in or out)
evidence_observation: Scope section talks about 'authorized targets, systems, networks, or applications' and Threat Actors are 'malicious insiders' — physical scope is neither in scope nor explicitly excluded.
evidence_expected: Each engagement SOP should explicitly mark physical scope as in-scope or out-of-scope with rationale (a Threat Scenario walkthrough on a server-room access control would be in scope, for example).
framework_refs: OSSTMM:OSSTMM §Physical Security testing
recommendation: Add one sentence in Scope stating that physical attacks are usually out of scope for Threat Scenarios (handled by PhysicalSocial_Process) unless the abused tool has a physical-access dimension.

title: Lessons-learned capture mechanism implicit — needs a concrete output target
evidence_observation: Closure mentions OFIs and the report but does not name where lessons-learned are captured for future scenarios (RT Research Repository, Confluence page, Jira label).
evidence_expected: Closure should explicitly route lessons-learned into a tracked store — e.g. RT Research Repository entry with a 'lessons-learned' tag.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Reporting — lessons learned
recommendation: Add a line to Closure: 'Lessons-learned must be filed in the RT Research Repository under the engagement codename with the lessons-learned tag.'

title: Physical scope not explicitly addressed in Operations SOP
evidence_observation: Introduction mentions 'a blended approach (Social Engineering, physical intrusion, application/network pentest)' but Research & Preparation, RT Test and Closure do not name physical scope decisions, controls or handovers.
evidence_expected: If physical attacks are in scope of an Operation, the SOP should cross-reference PhysicalSocial_Process v1.3 (Get-out-of-jail letter, Local CISO/Facilities involvement) or otherwise mark physical as out-of-scope by default.
framework_refs: OSSTMM:OSSTMM §Physical Security
recommendation: Add a sentence in Research & Preparation: 'If the Operation has a physical dimension, the PhysicalSocial_Process must be followed in parallel and a Get-out-of-jail letter prepared.'

title: Threat Intelligence Team not listed in Teams & Responsibilities (asymmetry with Exercises SOP)
evidence_observation: Operations v1.5 lists RT, BT, WT only. Research & Preparation references 'the TI team', but TI is not defined as a participating team. Exercises_Process v1.6 by contrast has a dedicated Threat Intelligence Team (TI) entry.
evidence_expected: Either (a) add a TI entry consistent with Exercises_Process v1.6, or (b) explicitly note that for Operations, TI work is performed inside the Red Team (no separate team) — both options resolve the cross-doc RACI conflict noted in references/corpus_vocabulary.yaml.
framework_refs: OTHER:Internal RACI consistency (references/corpus_vocabulary.yaml raci §Threat Intelligence work)
recommendation: Clarify TI team status in Operations Teams & Responsibilities to match the curated RACI.

title: Threat-hunt handover delegated to Purple Team close-out — could be explicit
evidence_observation: Closure references 'hunt-hypothesis list for the SOC' inside the Purple Team Close-Out subsection. There is no separate threat-hunt handover step (e.g. delivery to the threat-hunt function and SLA).
evidence_expected: An explicit threat-hunt handover step (recipient, deliverable, response SLA) prevents the hypothesis list from sitting in the close-out artefact and not being actioned.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Reporting / post-engagement actions
recommendation: Add a 'Threat-Hunt Handover' subsection naming the recipient (SOC threat-hunt function), the deliverable (hunt-hypothesis list) and a target acknowledgement SLA.

title: Data-handling rules present but GDPR / DORA wording not explicit
evidence_observation: Data Handling enumerates PIA, medical, justice, regulated data categories and a halt-on-encounter process, but does not name the legal basis (GDPR Art.6/9) nor any DORA risk-management-of-the-test reference.
evidence_expected: For audit-defensibility, the section should anchor sensitive-data handling to GDPR (legal basis) and to DORA Chapter IV operational-resilience-testing risk-management.
framework_refs: DORA:DORA Art.6/24 (general) — ICT risk management; testing risk controls; OTHER:GDPR Art.6 / Art.9 (general) — legal basis for processing sensitive data
recommendation: Add a one-line reference to GDPR legal basis and DORA risk-management-of-test obligations in Data Handling. Verify clause references against live regulation text.

title: Physical scope not explicitly addressed in Exercises SOP
evidence_observation: Introduction mentions 'a blended approach (Social Engineering, physical intrusion, application/network pentest)' but no phase names physical-scope decisions or handovers to PhysicalSocial_Process.
evidence_expected: Initiation should explicitly include physical scope (in / out) and, when in scope, defer to PhysicalSocial_Process v1.3 for execution-side controls (Get-out-of-jail letter, Local CISO/Facilities involvement).
framework_refs: OSSTMM:OSSTMM §Physical Security; TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — scoping (cyber and physical critical functions)
recommendation: Add a one-line entry in Initiation Phase outputs: 'Physical scope decision (in / out) — when in scope, follow PhysicalSocial_Process v1.3 in parallel.'

title: Threat-hunt handover only implicit (inside Purple Team close-out artefacts)
evidence_observation: Closure does not have a standalone threat-hunt handover step — only the hunt-hypothesis list inside the Purple Team close-out is mentioned.
evidence_expected: An explicit handover step (recipient, deliverable, response SLA) prevents the hypothesis list from sitting in the close-out artefact without being actioned.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Reporting; PTES:PTES Reporting — lessons learned
recommendation: Add a 'Threat-Hunt Handover' subsection naming the recipient, deliverable and SLA — mirrors recommendation in Operations finding.

title: TTI phase performed by 'the RT team' — RTS on TLPT requires independent TI provider for TLPT
evidence_observation: 'During this phase, the RT team focuses on gathering information about emerging and current threats, tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) employed by threat actors... Once the analysis is complete, the RT team shares the findings with the WT.'
evidence_expected: When the Exercise is run as TLPT, the TTI report must be authored by an independent TI provider (separate from the RT provider) per DORA Art.27 + RTS on TLPT.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 §Threat Intelligence — Targeted Threat Intelligence (TTI) authored by TI provider; DORA-TLPT:DORA Art.27 + RTS on TLPT — TI provider independence (verify live text)
recommendation: Add a 'When run as TLPT' callout: 'the Targeted Threat Intelligence report is authored by an independent TI provider distinct from the Red Team provider; the internal RT may participate in scoping and review only.'

title: White Team and Control Team conflated — TIBER-EU treats them as distinct roles
evidence_observation: White Team is described as: 'Link between the RT and the BT... Determining if, when and what information is shared with BT.' For an internal Exercise this collapses TIBER-EU's WT (broader oversight) and CT (small named-individual ownership of the test) into a single team.
evidence_expected: When TLPT-aligned, distinguish the Control Team (small, named individuals inside the institution who own the test) from the White Team (broader oversight). RTS on TLPT requires CT to be small and distinct.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 — Control Team vs White Team; DORA-TLPT:RTS on TLPT — Control Team definition (verify live text)
recommendation: Add a paragraph distinguishing CT (TLPT-mode) from WT (internal-mode). Note this is terminology drift not yet a regulatory breach — flag at low/medium until TLPT is actually scoped.

title: No Generic Threat Landscape (GTL) reference in Preparation
evidence_observation: Initiation and Targeted Threat Intelligence describe a TTI process but never reference the TIBER-EU Generic Threat Landscape (GTL) which feeds the TTI.
evidence_expected: TIBER-EU v2 Preparation phase consumes a GTL (jurisdiction-level) and uses it to derive TTI.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 §Preparation — Generic Threat Landscape consumption
recommendation: Add a sentence in Initiation referencing the relevant GTL (e.g. TIBER-NL / TIBER-BE GTL as appropriate for the jurisdiction).

title: Tracking forum left generic ('typically the Red Team Research Repository or the security risk register')
evidence_observation: 'Confirm the tracking forum where remediation actions will be logged (typically the Red Team Research Repository or the security risk register; record this decision in the workshop output.).'
evidence_expected: v1.0 should pin a single forum (or two clear forks: 'Red-team-owned remediations go in RT Research Repository; cross-team go in security risk register').
framework_refs: OTHER:Internal corpus consistency
recommendation: Pin forum at v1.0 once the Red Team Research Repository / security risk register split is agreed with risk function.

title: Only two teams listed (RT and BT) — Threat Hunt function not given a role despite hunt-hypothesis output
evidence_observation: 'Hunt-hypothesis list: actionable hypotheses for the SOC and Threat Hunt function...' but Teams & Responsibilities lists only RT and BT.
evidence_expected: Either (a) name a Threat Hunt sub-role inside BT (with the responsibility for consuming the hypothesis list), or (b) add a third entry for Threat Hunt / SOC.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Reporting / post-engagement
recommendation: Add a sentence in Teams & Responsibilities clarifying which role on the BT side owns the hunt-hypothesis list and the consumption SLA.

section_ref: Engagement Type-Specific notes / Physical & Social engagements
title: Physical & Social close-out attendee list missing (Facilities, Local CISO, Physical Security)
evidence_observation: Physical & Social engagement subsection describes detection-vs-action map against badge logs / CCTV / intruder-detection alarms but does not list the additional attendees required (Local CISO, Local Facilities Manager, Physical Security).
evidence_expected: Each Physical & Social close-out should explicitly add Local CISO and Local Facilities Manager as attendees — the same people who signed the Get-out-of-jail letter.
framework_refs: OSSTMM:OSSTMM §Physical Security — post-test review
recommendation: Add attendee list to Physical & Social subsection: Local CISO, Local Facilities Manager, Physical Security lead.

title: Doc never references TIBER-EU 360-degree feedback / DORA closure attestation
evidence_observation: Purple Teaming Process v0.1 describes the workshop, outputs and engagement-type specific notes but never names the TIBER-EU 360-degree feedback session, DORA closure attestation, Control Team or Threat Intelligence Provider — even though Exercises_Process v1.6 §Purple Team Close-Out routes TLPT-mode Exercises here.
evidence_expected: When the Exercise being closed out is a TLPT, the close-out is the TIBER-EU 360-degree feedback session and additional attestation artefacts are required (replay walkthrough, test report with ATT&CK coverage, joint attestation signed by RT Provider, TI Provider, Control Team and the financial entity). v1.0 should add a 'TLPT close-out' subsection.
framework_refs: TIBER-EU:TIBER-EU v2 §Closure — Replay, 360-degree feedback, attestation; DORA-TLPT:Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 Art.26-27 + RTS on TLPT — closure attestation (verify live text)
recommendation: Add a 'TLPT close-out' subsection in v1.0 mirroring the Exercises_Process v1.6 Purple Team Close-Out wording, naming Control Team, TI Provider, RT Provider and the financial entity as joint attestation signatories.

title: Hardware-implant inventory and recovery / clean-up steps not specified
evidence_observation: Escalation mentions 'connecting a rogue device to exposed ethernet ports, dropping rubber duckies or installing any other type of implants', but neither Closure nor a dedicated subsection describes implant inventory, retrieval, or sanitisation.
evidence_expected: Closure should require a hardware-implant register (asset, location dropped, retrieval status) and a retrieval / sanitisation step to prevent abandoned implants becoming a real attacker's foothold.
framework_refs: OSSTMM:OSSTMM §Physical Security — clean-up; PTES:PTES Post Exploitation — clean-up
recommendation: Add a 'Implant Inventory & Recovery' subsection to Closure (or Execution) — register every dropped item, who is to retrieve it, and confirmation when recovered.

title: OFIs and lessons-learned not explicitly required in Closure for Physical Pentest
evidence_observation: Closure narrates the report contents (recon, exploit, escalation, vulnerabilities, missing controls) but does not require an OFI block or a lessons-learned capture step — unlike Operations / Exercises / Threat Scenarios SOPs.
evidence_expected: Add OFIs (Red Team and Blue Team) and a lessons-learned capture step for consistency with the other SOPs.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Reporting — improvements / lessons learned
recommendation: Add OFI + lessons-learned to Closure with consistent wording.

title: Data handling rules absent for Physical & Social engagement
evidence_observation: No 'Data Handling' equivalent to Operations_Process v1.5 §Data Handling — yet physical pentests can incidentally capture badge data, CCTV recordings, employee personal data via social engineering, and sock-puppet account material.
evidence_expected: Add a 'Data Handling' subsection covering: (a) what to do if employees' personal data is captured, (b) handling of cloned-card data, (c) destruction of social-engineering pretext materials, (d) sock-puppet account decommissioning.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement — Handling of Sensitive Information; DORA:DORA general — data protection during testing (verify clause)
recommendation: Lift the Data Handling pattern from Operations_Process v1.5 and tailor for physical/social context (cloned cards, employee PII, sock-puppet account hygiene).

title: GDPR-grade data-protection rules absent for physical / social engineering captures
evidence_observation: Doc references Sock Puppet accounts and social-engineering captures (interacting with employees) but has no GDPR / data-protection treatment of the personal data inevitably gathered.
evidence_expected: DORA cross-references GDPR for personal data processed during testing; the SOP should reference GDPR Art.6/9 legal basis and document a retention / deletion policy for sock-puppet artefacts.
framework_refs: OTHER:GDPR Art.6 / Art.9 (general) — legal basis (verify); DORA:DORA general — interplay with GDPR (verify)
recommendation: Add a Data Handling subsection covering personal-data captures, sock-puppet account hygiene, badge-clone data destruction and retention. Verify clause references against the live regulation.

title: Discrepancy between this doc and PhysicalSocial_Process v1.3 on Questionnaire periodicity
evidence_observation: This doc says 'For offices marked as critical, the questionnaires will be sent, maximum, every twelve (12) months. For non-critical locations, they will be sent every thirty-six (36) months.' PhysicalSocial_Process v1.3 says 'the Questionnaire will be sent based on a base frequency (Fb) of 1 year, that will be adjusted with the maturity level of the location: the lower the maturity, the less spaced the Questionnaire will be sent.'
evidence_expected: The two docs should describe the same cadence in the same formula. Reconcile or have one defer to the other as authoritative.
framework_refs: OTHER:Internal corpus consistency
recommendation: Pick one doc as authoritative on Questionnaire cadence (the Questionnaire SOP is the natural home) and have the other reference it; align formulas.

title: No data handling rules for Questionnaire responses (which include photos, floor plans, CCTV info)
evidence_observation: Doc requests evidence including 'a photo of the door with the access control mechanism', 'pictures of the power equipment', 'pictures of alarm devices and panels', 'sample reports or screenshots' — yet there is no instruction on how these responses (which constitute sensitive physical-security intelligence) are stored, who has access, or retention.
evidence_expected: A 'Response Handling & Storage' subsection specifying encryption-at-rest, access control list, retention period and destruction policy for completed Questionnaires.
framework_refs: PTES:PTES Pre-engagement — Handling of Sensitive Information; DORA:DORA general — protection of confidential information collected during testing (verify)
recommendation: Add a Response Handling subsection specifying storage location, access (least-privilege), retention and destruction.

evidence_expected: Should specify: (a) who in SOC owns the suppression, (b) by what channel (deconfliction ticket / email), (c) how often the suppression is reconfirmed, (d) what happens if collection times or hosts change.

title: Continuous AD enumeration against prod could intersect DORA risk-management-of-testing — relationship not stated
evidence_observation: BloodHound continuously enumerates Active Directory in production via a service account with read on Deleted Objects and optional Local Admin on all domain-joined systems. Doc treats this as routine tooling.
evidence_expected: DORA Chapter IV (Art.24 general) requires risk management of operational-resilience testing. Continuous prod enumeration arguably sits at the boundary — should at minimum be cited in the team's testing risk register with a control-set (least-privilege service account, encrypted transport, suppression of alerts, monitored agent host).
framework_refs: DORA:DORA Art.24 (general) — risk management of operational resilience testing
recommendation: Add a 'Risk Management' note in v0.6 referencing the team risk register entry for continuous BloodHound collection. Verify clause against live DORA text.

recommendation: Expand Scoping into a fuller section with the dimensions above; keep the capability self-check as a sub-bullet.
